verdicts.reason: Lithuanian Dievas / Latvian Dievs is a direct reflex of PIE *Dyeus within Indo-European. This is a textbook cognate to the reconstructed etymon itself. Tier 1 is fully defensible.
Both Dievas and Greek Zeus continue PIE *Dyeus by regular sound-law within the Indo-European family. A classic established cognate. Tier 1 stands.
Iuppiter < *Dyeu-pater shares the *Dyeus root with Dievas; both Italic and Baltic are IE branches, so this is a proven cognate. Tier 1 defensible.
Vedic Dyaus(-pita) and Baltic Dievas both reflect PIE *Dyeus within Indo-European. Among the canonical cognate sets for the Sky-Father. Tier 1 holds.

Old Norse Tyr < Germanic *Tiwaz < PIE *Dyeus, the same etymon as Dievas; both Germanic and Baltic are IE. The name is a genuine cognate even though the role/rank diverged. Tier 1 defensible (the etymological claim is sound; the role-survival note is gloss, not the cognate basis).

Lithuanian Perkunas and Slavic Perun within the Balto-Slavic node; both plausibly reflect *Perk(w)unos. Closely related branches, and the thunder/oak association is shared. Defensible as a cognate, though it is the most-debated reflex pair — kept at tier 1 since both descend from a common Balto-Slavic/IE source.

Not a cognate claim but an intra-Baltic mythological PAIRING (thunder-god vs serpent/chthonic adversary) — the same tradition's living folklore, the IE thunder-vs-serpent combat. As an internal pairing within one tradition, tier-1 ('pairs') is appropriate; both figures are Baltic, no cross-family forgery risk.
Internal Baltic pairing: Sun-wife Saule and Moon-husband Menulis, their marriage/quarrel attested in the dainas. Same-tradition mythological dyad — tier-1 'pairs' is defensible.
Internal Baltic pairing within the celestial-marriage cycle: the Moon's courting of the dawn-star Ausrine. Same-tradition figures attested together in folklore. Tier-1 'pairs' holds.

Within the single Baltic tradition the morning-star Ausrine and the Sun Saule are attested together in the dainas as part of one sky-family cycle. As a same-tradition documented mythological relationship the tier is acceptable; this is an internal pairing rather than a cross-family link, so no forgery risk and no need to down-tier.

Internal Baltic pairing: the Dieva deli (Sons of God) court and rescue Saule's daughter / right her boat in the Sun-Maiden cycle. Same-tradition mythological dyad attested in Latvian dainas. Tier-1 'pairs' is defensible.

Internal Baltic relationship: the Dieva deli are named as the sons of Dievas, the Divine Twins (cf. *Diwos sunu / Vedic Divo napata, Greek Dioskouroi). Within the one tradition this is an attested mythological/genealogical pairing. Tier acceptable; same-tradition link, no cross-family over-claim.
